title: PROP-022 phase 5 + phase 6 detail amendment — resolve internal inconsistency, supply missing code-change specs, define regression tests
do_not_implement: DO NOT modify any state file, prompt, or script. Operator reviews and authorizes implementation per phase in subsequent commits.
summary: PROP-022's framework is sound — phases 1-3 shipped clean. This amendment fills in the per-phase specifics that were intentionally light in the original PROP-022 for the two highest-blast-radius migrations. Phase 5 (expansion-tracker.json, multi-writer, write-verify-coupled, structure-integrity-walked) gets 5 gap-resolutions with explicit before/after code blocks. Phase 6 (closed-issues.json, 4 readers, lookup-by-id critical) gets 4 gap-resolutions with grep-pattern verification, empirical wall-clock benchmarks, and a dual-write-window argument. Three cross-cutting design questions are answered: single-commit vs dual-write across both phases, integrity-side-check additions, and stale-prerequisite reconciliation. Empirical learnings from phases 1-3 inform every choice.

phases_already_shipped.approach: single-commit (3 files at once), no dual-write

learning_1_predicate_refinement_at_imple.what: Phase 1 found 1 record with `consumed:true` field but no `status` field — substantively terminal but kept 'live' by PROP-022's literal `status === 'pending'` predicate. Tinker correctly archived it but had to refine the predicate during the migration, not at PROP-author time.
learning_1_predicate_refinement_at_imple.implication_for_phase_5_and_6: Live-state predicates must be stated in terms of substance (where IS the item logically?) not just one canonical field. Phase 5's complete-vs-integrated discrimination (gap 1 below) is a textbook case where the canonical `status` field is insufficient and `integrated:true` carries the truer signal.

learning_2_inline_state_mutation_sites_a.what: Phase 3 found THREE distinct write sites in decider.md Step E2 that all had to change together: `pq.history=[]` init, two `pq.history.push(...)` calls, plus the `slice(-200)` cap removal. Buffer-and-append (`historyAppend[]` collected during the run, single `appendFileSync` at the end) replaced all three. PROP-022's per-file plan only mentioned 'replace pq.history.push() with appendFileSync' — the cleanup was broader.
learning_2_inline_state_mutation_sites_a.implication_for_phase_5_and_6: Audit each writer end-to-end before commit, don't just grep the canonical mutation. Phase 5 has multi-writer (analyst on completion, decider on integration, verifier on -pending-verification flip) with three separate sites; phase 6 has the same risk with the close-issues self-apply block (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md) plus the verifier's flip code.

learning_3_no_dual_write_was_needed.what: Phases 1-3 all shipped single-commit (backfill + writer-flip + reader-flip in one PR). No dual-write phase. Zero consumer regressions reported in tinker daily reports across 3 phases.
learning_3_no_dual_write_was_needed.implication_for_phase_5_and_6: The dual-write optionality in PROP-022 was conservative. Phases 1-3 prove the convention is sound enough to ship single-commit per-file. See dual_write_decision below — phase 5 single-commit; phase 6 single-commit gated on phases 1-5 staying clean for 7+ days post-phase-5.

learning_4_integrity_did_not_object.what: Daily structure-integrity 02:00Z runs found no issues across phases 1-3. The convention-doc-watch (state-file-archives.md) plus PROP-018 sync-manifest classifications were sufficient; no additional integrity-side check was needed.
learning_4_integrity_did_not_object.implication_for_phase_5_and_6: Phase 5 IS the case where structure-integrity has a load-bearing reader (collision detection in §7 of structure-integrity.md). The two-pass code change in this amendment is the additional integrity-side rigor PROP-022 anticipated. Phase 6 has no structure-integrity reader, so no equivalent change is needed there.

learning_5_next_id_field_was_already_pre.what: Priority-queue.json already had a `next_id` field used by decider-intake.md prediction-batch nextId computation. Migration just preserved it (switched from `Math.max(...history.map(...))` to `pq.next_id`). No counter rebuild needed.
learning_5_next_id_field_was_already_pre.implication_for_phase_5_and_6: expansion-tracker.json ALSO already has `next_id` (currently 292; documented in structure-integrity.md §7 as a Phase-0 invariant). closed-issues.json does NOT have a counter — IDs are generated by open-issues.json's allocator and only land in closed-issues at close-time. Neither phase needs a new counter. Phase 5 preserves expansion-tracker's existing next_id; phase 6 inherits open-issues.json's allocator unchanged.

phase_5_resolutions._intro: expansion-tracker.json — 5 gaps from DIRECTIVE-20260506-002 resolved below with explicit before/after code blocks, regression tests, and the multi-writer coordination story.

phase_5_gap_1_complete_status_inconsiste.gap: PROP-022's live_state_predicate says items[].status NOT in (complete, integrated, cancelled, superseded, subsumed) — i.e., 'complete' is TERMINAL. But reader_change says decider integration loop filters by status in (complete, revised) + !integrated — i.e., 'complete' is LIVE until integrated. Contradictory.
phase_5_gap_1_complete_status_inconsiste.audit_finding: Decider's actual code (decider.md line 114, decider-curmudgeon.md line 99): `t.items.filter(i=>(i.status==='complete'||i.status==='revised')&&!i.integrated)`. Decider's writer step (decider-curmudgeon.md line 130): 'Mark expansion `status: "complete"` AND `integrated: true` with `integrated_at` timestamp.' STATUS STAYS 'complete' AFTER INTEGRATION. The `integrated` boolean is the discriminator, NOT the status field. Same logic in step 2 line 106 (no_op confirmation): 'Just mark the tracker item integrated:true with integrated_at timestamp' — status not mentioned, stays as whatever it was.
phase_5_gap_1_complete_status_inconsiste.data_evidence: Snapshot 2026-05-06: 268 items have status='complete'. Of those, ~261 have integrated:true (terminal), ~7 don't (live, awaiting decider). EXP-001 has status='complete' AND integrated:true AND integrated_at='2026-04-07T08:53:00Z' — clearly terminal/archived semantically, but PROP-022's literal predicate would KEEP it in live. EXP-290 has status='pending' — also live. So 'status field' alone is insufficient to discriminate live from archive. Multiple status values (operator-approved, draft_awaiting_human_group_review, DRAFT_AWAITING_OPERATOR_REVIEW) also have items with `integrated:true` set later when decider integrates them; the status field never moves to 'integrated' in those cases either.
phase_5_gap_1_complete_status_inconsiste.resolution: **LIVE STATE PREDICATE (corrected):** `i.integrated !== true && i.status !== 'cancelled' && i.status !== 'superseded' && i.status !== 'subsumed'`. Equivalently: keep an item in live if `integrated:true` is NOT set AND its status is not one of the three terminal-by-status values. Move to archive when `integrated:true` is set OR when status flips to cancelled/superseded/subsumed.
phase_5_gap_1_complete_status_inconsiste.rationale: (a) The decider integration writer keeps status as 'complete' and only sets `integrated:true` — relying on status alone misses the integration event. (b) The decider integration READER filters by `!i.integrated` — it already treats `integrated` as the discriminator. (c) cancelled/superseded/subsumed are status-only terminals (decider/operator never sets `integrated:true` on them). (d) This matches the operator's lean stated in the directive: 'complete-but-not-yet-integrated stays in live until decider integrates.' Cleanly aligned.
writer_implications: Decider integration step (decider-curmudgeon.md line 130): in addition to setting integrated:true + integrated_at, NOW also append the full record to expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl AND remove from live items[]. This is the same atomic transaction.
Decider no_op step (decider-curmudgeon.md line 106): same — set integrated:true, append+remove.

Analyst cancel/supersede/subsume step (analyst.md): when status flips to cancelled/superseded/subsumed, append+remove. The analyst writer was already present pre-migration; it gains the archive-append.

Verifier (verify-pending-state.js, integrated-pending-verification → integrated): when the verifier flips status to 'integrated' (from 'integrated-pending-verification'), it MUST also set integrated:true (currently it only changes status), AND append+remove. See gap 3 below for the explicit verifier code change.
phase_5_gap_1_complete_status_inconsiste.open_question_resolved: The 'complete' status itself is no longer a discriminator. Future EXPs from analyst start with status='pending' or 'complete' (analyst's choice depending on workflow); decider stamps integrated:true on integration; only at THAT moment does the item become archive. Items with status='complete' but no integrated:true STAY LIVE indefinitely — that's correct, they need decider attention. Operator can audit via `node -e "const t=JSON.parse(...); const stuck=t.items.filter(i=>i.status==='complete'&&!i.integrated); console.log(stuck.length+' items awaiting integration')"`.

phase_5_gap_2_structure_integrity_two_pa.gap: PROP-022 said 'collision detection MUST iterate both live + archive — code change required: replace items.find(...) with two-pass over live and archive.' No explicit code-change spec.
phase_5_gap_2_structure_integrity_two_pa.audit_finding: structure-integrity.md §7 has FIVE checks that walk expansion-tracker.json (not just one find()). Each needs a separate code-change story:

checks_inventory.current_code_style: Extract sorted EXP-NNN IDs from `t.items`, scan for gaps in numerical sequence.
checks_inventory.post_migration_change: Walk LIVE items[] AND streamed archive lines. Union the IDs. Sort numerically. Scan for gaps. The check is GLOBAL — gaps anywhere (live or archive) are bugs.
checks_inventory.wall_clock: Empirical benchmark on 280-line archive: 19ms total (read+parse+sort+scan). Daily run, well below structure-integrity's typical step latency.

checks_inventory.post_migration_change: max must include archive IDs. Otherwise an item that was just integrated (moved to archive) could leave live's max < next_id, masking a real allocator bug.
checks_inventory.wall_clock: Same as ID-continuity check — single read of archive, no extra walk.

checks_inventory.current_code_style: List files in `monitor/analyst/expansions/`. For each EXP-NNN.json, verify a matching tracker entry. Flag orphans.
checks_inventory.post_migration_change: Tracker entry can now be in EITHER live or archive. Two-pass lookup.
checks_inventory.wall_clock: Single archive read (cached if used adjacent to other §7 checks). 19ms-ish.

checks_inventory.check_name: Phantom tracker entries (entry references missing output_file)
checks_inventory.current_code_style: Walk t.items[] filtered by status:complete + has output_file; verify file exists.
checks_inventory.post_migration_change: Walk LIVE only. Archive entries are by definition past — they were verified at integration time, no need to re-verify file existence on every integrity run. (Operator's call — could be made global if file-deletion is a concern. Current default: live-only.)
checks_inventory.after_code:
// Unchanged — live-only walk. Archive entries excluded by design (already verified at integration time, immutable past).
// If operator wants global verification later, add a second loop over the archive lines.

checks_inventory.check_name: Coupled-category coverage (category in section-new/category-proposal-writeup AND status!=='integrated' must have priority-queue entry)
checks_inventory.post_migration_change: After migration the only items with these categories that AREN'T archived are the ones still pending integration. The filter `status !== 'integrated'` becomes equivalent to 'in live items'. NO archive read needed — once integrated, the requirement to be on the priority queue is moot. So this check stays live-only.

phase_5_gap_2_structure_integrity_two_pa.where_in_structure_integrity_md: All five checks are in §7 (lines ~143-204) of monitor/prompts/structure-integrity.md. The amendment touches three of the five checks (ID continuity, next_id correctness, orphaned output files); two stay unchanged (phantom entries, coupled-category — both correctly limited to live). Phase 5's PR adjusts the three changed code blocks plus a 1-line preamble note: 'Post-PROP-022-phase-5: ID continuity + next_id correctness + orphan detection iterate live AND archive. Phantom-entry and coupled-category checks remain live-only by design.'
phase_5_gap_2_structure_integrity_two_pa.streaming_vs_full_load_decision: Full-load (split('\n')) is correct for archive sizes <10MB (the rotation threshold). Today's archive after backfill: ~280 items × ~1KB/item ≈ 280KB. At ~50 new integrations/year, growth is ~50KB/year — 100+ years to hit the 10MB rotation threshold. Streaming via readline adds complexity (async/sync mismatch with the rest of structure-integrity's sync code) for no measurable benefit at these sizes. Document in state-file-archives.md§reader-discipline: 'For archives under 10MB, readFileSync + split is acceptable. Switch to readline streaming only at rotation time.'

phase_5_gap_3_discipline_1a_verify_code.gap: PROP-022 said 'Discipline 1a write-verify: confirm integrated_at field appears in archive line within 1 cycle of the integration commit (push-verify reads archive grep for the EXP id).' No explicit code-change spec for verify-pending-state.js.
phase_5_gap_3_discipline_1a_verify_code.audit_finding: verify-pending-state.js scans `closed-issues.json` AND `expansion-tracker.json` (lines 70-73 of the script). The SCAN_TARGETS array drives both. For expansion-tracker, the script's filter is `status` matches /-pending-verification$/; if matched and verification passes, status flips to TERMINAL_MAP[status] (e.g., 'integrated-pending-verification' → 'integrated'). Currently the script ONLY mutates the status field + verified_at + verified_by_run + verifier_script_version (line 170-173). Post-migration, the script must ALSO set integrated:true (the new authoritative discriminator from gap 1) AND archive-append + remove-from-live.

two_options_considered.option: A — verifier does the archive-move atomically
two_options_considered.pro: Cleanest. The flip and the archive-move happen in one transaction. No 'flipped-but-not-yet-archived' intermediate state polluting the live file.
two_options_considered.con: verify-pending-state.js gains write-side knowledge of the archive convention. The script becomes more complex.
two_options_considered.cost: ~25 LOC addition: detect file path → archive sibling, on flip-to-terminal append-and-remove, write live file once at end.

two_options_considered.option: B — verifier flips status only, decider sweeps next run
two_options_considered.con: Live file accumulates 'integrated' items for up to one decider cycle (~4h). Breaks the live_state_predicate temporarily. Decider's integration loop filters by `!i.integrated` so it WOULDN'T sweep these — would need a separate 'flipped-by-verifier' sweep step. Adds complexity elsewhere instead of in the verifier.
two_options_considered.cost: ~15 LOC addition in decider, but at the cost of a temporal hole in the live-state predicate.

phase_5_gap_3_discipline_1a_verify_code.decision: Option A. Verifier atomicity is the right place for this — the verifier IS the authoritative writer at the moment status flips to terminal. Decider's responsibility is integration, not cleanup-of-flipped-pending-verification entries.

exact_code_change_to_verify_pending_stat.version_bump: Bump VERSION constant to '1.1.0' (line 54). Reason: backward-compatible behavior change — verifier now archives flipped entries when archive_path is provided. Old report consumers see new `archived` field in per_file totals; not breaking.

discipline_1a_push_verify_in_decider.context: decider.md line 13 already documents the discipline: 'before writing integrated:true to expansion-tracker.json, verify the push succeeded. If push 403'd, leave status as integrated-pending-verification with verification_pattern field.'
discipline_1a_push_verify_in_decider.no_change_needed_in_decider: The decider's behavior on successful push is: stamp integrated:true, append+remove to archive, push. On 403: leave entry in LIVE with status='integrated-pending-verification' (no integrated:true yet, no archive append yet). Workspace-sync's verify-pending-state.js then takes over per the after-code above.
discipline_1a_push_verify_in_decider.verification_pattern_format: decider continues to write `verification_pattern: "git show origin/main:<patch_file> | grep -qF '<fingerprint>'"`. The pattern grep matches against the canonical content target (data/wins.json or data/sections.json), not the tracker file itself. Tracker file mutations (status flip, archive move) happen on verify-pending-state.js's pass. Unchanged from current discipline.

phase_5_gap_4_id_generation.gap: Does expansion-tracker.json have a next_id field? PROP-022 didn't specify; if it does, post-migration we need to preserve it.
phase_5_gap_4_id_generation.audit_finding: YES. expansion-tracker.json has `next_id` (currently 292). Documented as a Phase-0 invariant in structure-integrity.md §7. The decider intake allocator (decider-curmudgeon.md lines 184-191 and 222-231) reads and increments t.next_id. If next_id is missing or non-numeric, both code sites self-heal by computing `t.items.reduce((m,i)=>Math.max(m, parseInt(i.id...)),0)+1` and writing it back, with a LOUD warning.
phase_5_gap_4_id_generation.resolution: next_id stays in the LIVE file post-migration (it's metadata, not an item record). Same precedent as priority-queue.json phase 3 (pq.next_id stayed in the live file). Implementation: backfill script preserves next_id; writers continue to read+increment t.next_id from the live file.
phase_5_gap_4_id_generation.self_heal_change_needed: The two self-heal blocks in decider-curmudgeon.md must additionally include archive max in the recompute. Otherwise: live array post-migration could be mostly empty after a wave of integrations; max(live) drops; if next_id is somehow lost, self-heal would set next_id = max(live)+1, potentially BELOW recently-archived IDs → collision.
phase_5_gap_4_id_generation.pre_push_integrity_gate_change: decider-patches-and-selfapply.md lines 219-228 has Invariant 1 of the pre-push gate. It already does `next_id > max_id` on live items only. Same change as the self-heal: include archive max. Code in gap 2's next_id-correctness sub-block above.

phase_5_gap_5_regression_test.gap: PROP-022's risk_callout for phase 5 acknowledged this is load-bearing but didn't specify how to verify post-migration. Define pre-migration baseline + post-migration assertions.

test_plan._intro: Five one-shot pre/post assertions. Each captures specific behavior pre-migration, runs the migration, then verifies post-migration produces equivalent results. Run as `tests/phase5-regression.sh` invoked once during the migration commit (not recurring).

test_1_collision_detection_baseline.purpose: Confirm structure-integrity §7 collision detection (gap 2) finds the same set of IDs post-migration.
test_1_collision_detection_baseline.post_migration_assertion:
```bash
node -e "const fs=require('fs');const t=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json','utf8'));const liveIds=t.items.map(i=>parseInt((i.id||'EXP-0').replace('EXP-',''))||0);const archIds=fs.readFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl','utf8').split('\n').filter(Boolean).map(l=>parseInt((JSON.parse(l).id||'EXP-0').replace('EXP-',''))||0);const ids=[...liveIds,...archIds].sort((a,b)=>a-b);const s={total:ids.length,min:ids[0],max:ids[ids.length-1],distinct:new Set(ids).size};const pre=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('/tmp/phase5-pre-collision.json','utf8').split('\n')[0]);if(s.total!==pre.total||s.min!==pre.min||s.max!==pre.max||s.distinct!==pre.distinct){console.error('FAIL: id-set mismatch',s,'vs',pre);process.exit(1)}console.log('PASS: id-set preserved')"
```
test_1_collision_detection_baseline.fail_action: Abort migration. Restore expansion-tracker.json from pre-migration backup. Diagnose backfill bug.

test_2_decider_integration_loop_input_un.purpose: The decider integration loop filter `(status==='complete'||status==='revised') && !integrated` must return the SAME set of items pre and post migration (since live file should still contain exactly those items).
test_2_decider_integration_loop_input_un.post_migration_assertion:
```bash
node -e "const fs=require('fs');const t=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('monitor/analyst/expansion-tracker.json','utf8'));const c=t.items.filter(i=>(i.status==='complete'||i.status==='revised')&&!i.integrated);const post={count:c.length,ids:c.map(i=>i.id).sort()};const pre=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('/tmp/phase5-pre-integration.json','utf8'));if(JSON.stringify(post.ids)!==JSON.stringify(pre.ids)){console.error('FAIL: integration loop input changed',post,'vs',pre);process.exit(1)}console.log('PASS: '+post.count+' items still queued for integration')"
```

test_3_analyst_dispatcher_input_unchange.purpose: Analyst dispatcher precheck filters by status='pending' + !blocked_on. Must be unchanged post-migration (pending items aren't archived).

test_4_verify_pending_baseline_no_op.purpose: Confirm verify-pending-state.js still passes on the post-migration tree. There may be no integrated-pending-verification entries today; verify still completes cleanly.
test_4_verify_pending_baseline_no_op.post_migration_assertion:
```bash
node monitor/scripts/verify-pending-state.js --dry-run > /tmp/phase5-post-verify.txt 2>&1
# verifier exit 0; checked counts may differ if a real flip happened mid-migration; but no errors
grep -E 'errors=0' /tmp/phase5-post-verify.txt && echo PASS || { echo FAIL; cat /tmp/phase5-post-verify.txt; exit 1; }
```

test_5_round_trip_integration.purpose: Synthetic test: stamp a known live item with integrated:true via the decider writer pattern, verify it lands in archive and is removed from live.

phase_5_gap_5_regression_test.out_of_band_smoke_test: After migration commit lands, watch for 7 days: (a) tinker daily Mode 1 finds no stranded patches; (b) structure-integrity §7 produces zero new findings; (c) decider integration loop count > 0 if any live 'complete' items existed pre-migration; (d) verify-pending-state.js continues to run cleanly. Three consecutive clean tinker daily reports = phase 5 considered shipped.

phase_6_resolutions._intro: closed-issues.json — 4 gaps from DIRECTIVE-20260506-002 resolved below. Phase 6 is the highest-blast-radius migration: 4 readers, 1.4MB file, lookup-by-id critical. Each gap addressed with empirical benchmarks and call-site verification.

phase_6_gap_1_stale_prerequisite_reconci.gap: PROP-022 phase 6 risk_callout said 'Validate convention on all 10 prior files before approaching closed-issues.' But phasing has 5 files in phases 1-3 (done), 3 files in phase 4 (next), 1 file in phase 5 (this amendment), then closed-issues. The '10 prior files' framing is from the original equal-weighting view.
phase_6_gap_1_stale_prerequisite_reconci.resolution: Phase 6 prerequisite is: **phases 1-5 complete + 7 days of clean operation post-phase-5 commit**. NOT phases 1-4 + 7 days. The 7-day window starts the day phase 5 (expansion-tracker, the previous load-bearing migration) commits. This matches the operator's lean stated in the directive.
rationale: Phase 5 is the migration most structurally similar to phase 6: multi-writer + lookup-readers + load-bearing-consumer (structure-integrity for phase 5; decider lifecycle verifier for phase 6). If phase 5 ships clean for 7 days, that's the strongest possible signal that phase 6's pattern works.

Phases 1-4 (human-notes × 4 + priority-queue + attention-inbox + tracker.json + workspace-sync-skips rotation) are all SIMPLER patterns: status-filter readers OR single-writer OR already-archive-shaped. They don't stress the convention the way phase 5 will.

7 days = 4 × decider cadence (current ~1.5h) × ~28 cycles + 7 × structure-integrity daily runs + 14+ × workspace-sync hourly verifier runs. If anything is going to surface a regression, it surfaces in the first week.

phase_6_gap_2_grep_pattern_correctness.gap: PROP-022's reader code uses `grep '"id":"${N}"'` to find an issue in the JSONL archive. Verify against actual call sites: do consumers reference issues with ISS-NNN prefix or as bare integer?

audit_findings_per_call_site.location: monitor/prompts/analyst.md line 129 (Mode 2 attention-inbox triage, OBE Phase 1)
audit_findings_per_call_site.current_behavior: Regex `\bISS-\d+\b` extracts the FULL `ISS-NNN` token from the item's `reason` field. Look up that token in closed-issues.json: `c.issues.find(i => i.id === 'ISS-NNN')`. The lookup uses the FULL ISS-prefix string, not a bare integer.
audit_findings_per_call_site.post_migration_grep_pattern: `grep '"id":"ISS-NNN"'` — works as written in PROP-022. The pattern matches the JSONL serialization of the id field exactly: `{"id":"ISS-NNN",...}` (no space after colon — JSON.stringify default).

audit_findings_per_call_site.location: monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md line 127 (Step 8a batch-triage, batchability gate condition 1)
audit_findings_per_call_site.current_behavior: Reads `context_hints.related_issues` array (each entry is full ISS-NNN string). For each ISS-NNN, finds closure record in closed-issues.json and reads severity field.
audit_findings_per_call_site.post_migration_grep_pattern: Same pattern: `grep '"id":"ISS-NNN"'`. For multiple IDs (the typical batch case), can use grep -E with alternation OR grep -F -f patternfile (both ≤2ms on 1.4MB; ≤3ms on 5MB — see benchmark below).

audit_findings_per_call_site.post_migration_change: `wc -l monitor/decisions/closed-issues-archive.jsonl`. NOTE: with event-sourcing semantics (status updates append new lines), wc -l counts ALL events, not unique issues. If decider needs unique-issue count, use `awk -F'"id":"' '{print $2}' archive | awk -F'"' '{print $1}' | sort -u | wc -l` instead. Recommendation: keep wc -l (event count) unless the use case demands unique-issue count; document the distinction in DATA-SCHEMAS.md.
audit_findings_per_call_site.caveat: Event count vs unique-issue count diverge once status updates start appending new lines. PROP-022's spec implied unique-issue count without saying so. Operator chooses: cheap event-count OR slightly slower unique-count. Phase 6 commit message should pick one and document in DATA-SCHEMAS.md.

audit_findings_per_call_site.current_behavior: On self-apply: append closing record to c.issues[], full file rewrite of closed-issues.json. On verify: SCAN_TARGETS reads closed-issues.json, walks issues[], filters by status matching /-pending-verification$/, runs verification_pattern, on pass mutates entry's status field.
audit_findings_per_call_site.post_migration_change_for_writer: decider-patches-and-selfapply.md: replace `c.issues.push(issue); fs.writeFileSync('monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json', ...)` with `fs.appendFileSync('monitor/decisions/closed-issues-archive.jsonl', JSON.stringify(issue) + '\n')`. The whole 1.4MB serialization-on-every-close goes away. See gap 4 (dual-write decision) for whether to keep the live writeFileSync during a transition window.
audit_findings_per_call_site.post_migration_change_for_verifier: verify-pending-state.js's SCAN_TARGETS for closed-issues changes shape: post-phase-6, closed-issues.json IS the archive (no separate live file with a small array). The verifier needs to walk the archive's last-event-per-id (event-sourcing tail-1 pattern) for entries whose latest event has status matching /-pending-verification$/. That's a non-trivial code change. Recommendation: same approach as phase 5 — verifier becomes archive-aware, SCAN_TARGETS gains a 4th tuple element for the file's archive_path, and the read code branches on whether the file is JSON-array-shaped vs JSONL-shaped. Detail in code-change-spec below.
audit_findings_per_call_site.caveat: verify-pending-state.js needs both writer-side change (append-to-archive on flip) AND reader-side change (read JSONL archive, group by id, select tail-1 per id, filter by pending-verification status). Phase 6's verifier change is the largest single code-change in the migration.

audit_findings_per_call_site.current_behavior: Prose instruction (no code template): 'check BOTH open-issues.json AND closed-issues.json before filing.' The agent (Sonnet) writes the lookup code at runtime.
audit_findings_per_call_site.post_migration_change: Update social.md prose to instruct grep against archive: 'check open-issues.json (read JSON, scan issues[]) AND closed-issues-archive.jsonl (grep `"id":"ISS-NNN"` and JSON.parse the matching line, OR grep for keyword in description if the candidate has no ID yet).' Provide a concrete grep template in the prompt so the agent doesn't have to invent it.

empirical_benchmark._method: Synthesized 5MB JSONL from current closed-issues.json (1518 lines × 4 = 6072 lines, 4.9MB). Measured grep + JSON.parse latency. All measurements on workspace sandbox, default ext4 cache cold->warm.
empirical_benchmark.speedup_factor: ~10-20x for single lookup; ~10x for bulk on JSONL
empirical_benchmark.comparison_basis: JSON.parse of 1.4MB requires loading entire file + building object tree; grep streams bytes.

phase_6_gap_2_grep_pattern_correctness.id_format_in_jsonl_will_match_grep_patte: Verified by node -e "console.log(JSON.stringify({id:'ISS-001',foo:'bar'}))" → `{"id":"ISS-001","foo":"bar"}`. JSON.stringify (default, no space) produces `"id":"ISS-001"` exactly — no space after colon. This is what writers will produce via `appendFileSync(path, JSON.stringify(record) + '\n')`. Grep pattern `"id":"ISS-001"` matches. The pretty-printed `"id": "ISS-001"` (with space) only appears in the current pretty-printed closed-issues.json — that file goes away post-migration.

phase_6_gap_3_acceptance_wallclock_thres.gap: Phase 6 said 'Wall-clock comparable (grep + JSON.parse 1 line vs JSON.parse-1.4MB)' but no acceptance threshold. Specify with empirical numbers.

thresholds.single_issue_lookup_5MB_archive: ≤10ms — well above measured 2-3ms; allows for FS cache miss + node spawn overhead
thresholds.bulk_50_issue_lookup_5MB_archive: ≤50ms — used by analyst's worst-case OBE pass with many ISS references

phase_6_gap_3_acceptance_wallclock_thres.acceptance_test:
```bash
# tests/phase6-perf.sh — run pre-commit; fail commit if any threshold exceeded.
set -e
ARCHIVE=monitor/decisions/closed-issues-archive.jsonl
[ -f "$ARCHIVE" ] || { echo 'archive not yet created — run after backfill'; exit 0; }
SIZE_MB=$(du -m "$ARCHIVE" | cut -f1)
echo "Archive size: ${SIZE_MB}MB"
# Single lookup
T=$(node -e "const t=Date.now(); require('child_process').execSync('grep \"\\\"id\\\":\\\"ISS-100\\\"\" $ARCHIVE | head -1', {encoding:'utf8'}); console.log(Date.now()-t)")
echo "single-lookup: ${T}ms"
[ $T -le 10 ] || { echo 'FAIL: single-lookup exceeded 10ms'; exit 1; }
# Bulk 5
T=$(node -e "const t=Date.now(); require('child_process').execSync('grep -E \"\\\"id\\\":\\\"(ISS-100|ISS-200|ISS-300|ISS-400|ISS-500)\\\"\" $ARCHIVE', {encoding:'utf8'}); console.log(Date.now()-t)")
echo "bulk-5-lookup: ${T}ms"
[ $T -le 15 ] || { echo 'FAIL: bulk-5 exceeded 15ms'; exit 1; }
# wc -l
T=$(node -e "const t=Date.now(); require('child_process').execSync('wc -l $ARCHIVE', {encoding:'utf8'}); console.log(Date.now()-t)")
echo "wc-l: ${T}ms"
[ $T -le 5 ] || { echo 'FAIL: wc -l exceeded 5ms'; exit 1; }
echo 'PASS: all phase 6 perf thresholds met'
```
phase_6_gap_3_acceptance_wallclock_thres.headroom: Today's archive will be ~1.4MB at backfill time. Measured ~2-3ms per lookup. 10ms threshold gives 3-5x headroom for the projected 5MB-archive (~3 months of growth at current closure rate ~10/day). At 10MB rotation threshold, lookups would still be ~5-6ms — comfortable. Threshold is intentionally loose to avoid CI flakes from FS cache cold-start; tighten in a follow-up PROP if metrics warrant.

phase_6_gap_4_dual_write_window_argument.gap: PROP-022 said 'Dual-write phase: writer appends to archive AND keeps in live array for one full week. All four lookup readers fall back to live if archive grep returns nothing.' Phases 1-3 all skipped this. For phase 6's higher blast radius, is the 7-day dual-write phase still warranted?
argument_for_skipping_dual_write: Phases 1-3 prove the convention is sound. Zero consumer regressions across 5 files.
Phase 5 will further validate the convention with the highest-complexity migration (multi-writer + verifier-aware + structure-integrity-walked).
The 7-day dual-write window doubles operator overhead (two writes per close, two reads per lookup) and adds ~13 days of weighted-cost while waiting (current weighted reads × 1.4MB live).
The consumer-protection_matrix in PROP-022 already preserves every reader explicitly; we're not relying on dual-write as the safety net.
All four lookup readers can be tested pre-commit via the regression test plan (capture pre-migration outputs for known IDs; assert post-migration outputs match).
argument_for_keeping_dual_write: Phase 6 has 4 readers, the most of any migrated file. Phase 5 has 4 readers but less lookup-by-id critical.

decider lifecycle verification (verify-pending-state.js) is non-trivial — gap 2 noted this is the largest single code-change. A 7-day window catches verifier bugs before they corrupt the verifier's mutation log.
Event-sourcing semantics (status updates append new lines, readers tail -1 per id) is new — first migration to use it. Worth a window for early bugs.
1.4MB archive growing at +1MB/year — even a regression that requires re-backfill is recoverable from git history, but shipping a regression has higher reputational cost than dual-write overhead.
phase_6_gap_4_dual_write_window_argument.decision: **Skip dual-write IF phase 5 ships clean for 7+ days. Keep dual-write OPTION available as a fallback if phase 5 shows ANY regressions (even minor ones).**
phase_6_gap_4_dual_write_window_argument.concrete_gate: On phase 5 commit + 7 days: re-evaluate. If tinker daily reports + integrity reports are clean → phase 6 ships single-commit (no dual-write). If ANY phase-5-related finding surfaced (even moderate) → phase 6 uses 7-day dual-write window as belt-and-suspenders.
phase_6_gap_4_dual_write_window_argument.operator_decision_authorities: Operator decides at phase-6-PR-author time based on phase 5's track record. Tinker recommends single-commit by default given phases 1-3 cleanliness; switches recommendation to dual-write if phase 5 trips.

cross_cutting_observations_from_directiv.operator_correctable_schema_mismatch_pha: Real (1 record with consumed:true but no status — see learning_1 above). Predicates must address substance, not just canonical-field. Phase 5's gap 1 explicitly acknowledges this with the integrated:true discriminator.
cross_cutting_observations_from_directiv.writers_inline_state_mutation_patterns: Real (3+ sites in decider.md Step E2 — see learning_2). Phase 5 has multi-writer with at least 4 sites: analyst on cancel/supersede (analyst.md), decider on integration (decider-curmudgeon.md), decider on no_op (same), verify-pending-state.js on verifier flip. All four sites identified above.
cross_cutting_observations_from_directiv.integrity_did_not_object_to_phase_1_3: Real (no findings in daily 02:00Z runs across phases 1-3). Phase 5 introduces the FIRST integrity-side reader change (structure-integrity §7). Phase 6 has no integrity reader; verify-pending-state.js is the load-bearing consumer.
cross_cutting_observations_from_directiv.files_already_excluded: Confirmed: phases 5 + 6 do NOT touch monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*.json (PROP-019/020 fingerprint compare). Confirmed in non_goals below.

q1_single_commit_vs_dual_write_phase_5.question: For phase 5: single-commit (like phases 1-3) or dual-write/dual-read 7-day phase?
q1_single_commit_vs_dual_write_phase_5.rationale: Phases 1-3 prove the convention works without dual-write. Phase 5's higher complexity (multi-writer, verifier-aware, structure-integrity-walked) is offset by: (a) explicit code-change specs in this amendment for every consumer; (b) regression-test plan in gap 5 captures pre-migration baselines for direct comparison; (c) the verifier's archive-aware change is contained to one script (verify-pending-state.js). Dual-write would double the writer overhead during the window AND add a fallback read path that needs its own removal commit. Single-commit is simpler and the phase-1-3 track record supports it.
q1_single_commit_vs_dual_write_phase_5.fallback: If phase 5's pre-commit regression tests fail, switch to a smaller-scope migration: ship the structure-integrity changes (gap 2) FIRST as a no-op (read both live + archive even though archive is empty; produces same results), then the writer changes (decider + analyst + verifier) in a SECOND commit. This decouples reader and writer changes if needed. NOT recommended unless tests fail — adds an extra commit and PR for limited benefit.

q2_single_commit_vs_dual_write_phase_6.question: For phase 6: same question, with higher blast radius weighing in.
q2_single_commit_vs_dual_write_phase_6.answer: SINGLE COMMIT, GATED ON PHASE 5 STABILITY.
q2_single_commit_vs_dual_write_phase_6.rationale: See gap 4 above. Phase 6 ships single-commit IF phase 5 has been stable for 7+ days post-commit. If phase 5 had any phase-5-related findings (even moderate), phase 6 uses the 7-day dual-write window. The dual-write spec in PROP-022 stays valid as the documented fallback; this amendment doesn't remove it, just makes it conditional on phase 5's track record.
q2_single_commit_vs_dual_write_phase_6.fallback: Per gap 4: dual-write 7-day window with reader fallback to live if archive grep returns empty. The fallback code is already in PROP-022 — preserve it as commented-out backup in case phase 5 trips.

q3_integrity_side_checks_to_add.question: For both phases: are there integrity-side checks that should be added (or strengthened) to catch a regression that PROP-022's existing convention-doc-watch wouldn't catch?
q3_integrity_side_checks_to_add.answer: YES, for phase 5 only. Phase 6 needs no integrity-side check.

phase_5_check_to_add.what: Add a §7e to structure-integrity.md: 'Live/archive ID disjointness for expansion-tracker.' Walk live items[] and archive lines; compute set intersection. Any ID appearing in BOTH live AND archive is a regression — likely an integration that didn't remove from live, OR a re-allocation of a previously-archived ID.
phase_5_check_to_add.severity: Major. ID overlap means either: (a) the writer didn't atomically remove from live, OR (b) the next_id allocator re-issued a previously-archived ID. Both are bugs that the §7 next_id check (gap 2 above) might miss if archive's max_id is below live's. Disjointness is the strict invariant.
phase_5_check_to_add.ship_with_phase_5: YES — add this check in the same commit as the structure-integrity.md changes from gap 2.

phase_6_check_not_needed.rationale: closed-issues has no live counterpart post-migration (it IS the archive). There's no live-archive disjointness to check. The verify-pending-state.js script enforces idempotency and append-only semantics by design. Convention-doc-watch on state-file-archives.md is sufficient.

non_goals: Do NOT redesign the unified archive convention. PROP-022's convention has shipped successfully on 5 files; keep it.
Do NOT broaden scope to phase 4 (attention-inbox, curmudgeon/tracker, workspace-sync-skips rotation). Operator handles that with current PROP-022 spec.
Do NOT propose changes to monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/*.json (PROP-019/020 fingerprint compare, off-limits).
Do NOT propose schema changes beyond what's needed for the live/archive split. The expansion-tracker and closed-issues schemas stay otherwise unchanged.
Do NOT speculate beyond phase 6. Future state files are governed by the convention reference doc.
Do NOT add a new dispatcher mode or scheduled task. All work is per-file migration on operator authorization.

implementation_order_recommendation.phase_5_a: Backfill script + state-file-archives.md table update for expansion-tracker (1 commit).
implementation_order_recommendation.phase_5_b: Writer changes (decider-curmudgeon.md integration step + analyst.md cancel/supersede + verify-pending-state.js v1.1.0) + structure-integrity.md §7 + new §7e disjointness check + decider-patches-and-selfapply.md pre-push gate fix (1 commit, can be combined with phase_5_a or split).
implementation_order_recommendation.wait_7_days: Operator monitors tinker daily + integrity daily for findings.
implementation_order_recommendation.phase_6_gate_check: Run gate command from gap 1 above. GO → proceed; NO-GO → diagnose, postpone, or switch to dual-write.
implementation_order_recommendation.phase_6_a: Backfill closed-issues into closed-issues-archive.jsonl + state-file-archives.md table update (1 commit).
implementation_order_recommendation.phase_6_b: Writer changes (decider-patches-and-selfapply.md self-apply block + verify-pending-state.js v1.2.0 with archive-walk + reader changes across analyst.md / curmudgeon.md / decider-intake.md / social.md / decider-curmudgeon.md) + DATA-SCHEMAS.md schema update (1 commit, OR split into reader-only and writer-only as risk-mitigation).
implementation_order_recommendation.post_phase_6: Tinker Mode 3 final verification: confirm closed-issues.json removed (or sentinel-only), archive size matches expected, all 4 readers tested. Mark PROP-022 fully shipped.

completion_criteria.file_present: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-022-amendment-001.json exists with phase_5_resolutions and phase_6_resolutions sections fully addressed.
completion_criteria.phase_5_gaps_resolved: All 5 phase 5 gaps have explicit answers; the complete-status inconsistency is resolved with code-grounded reasoning.
completion_criteria.phase_6_gaps_resolved: All 4 phase 6 gaps have explicit answers; the grep-pattern verification is concrete (5 specific call sites checked).
completion_criteria.code_change_specs_explicit: Every consumer touched in phases 5 and 6 has an explicit before/after code block. ✓ (5 in gap 5.2, 1 in gap 5.3, 1 in gap 5.4, 5 in gap 6.2)
completion_criteria.regression_tests_defined: Phase 5 has a 5-test regression plan; phase 6 has wall-clock thresholds + an acceptance test script.
completion_criteria.open_questions_addressed: All 3 cross-cutting design questions answered with reasoning, not deferred.
